body: tracker.fsck() defines a nested visit() function that recursively calls itself to walk dependencies (tracker.py ~line 1186-1211). Python's default recursion limit is ~1000; a chain of ~1000+ linked tickets would raise RecursionError inside fsck. While unlikely in most trackers, fsck is a 'last line of defence' integrity check and should not itself fail on deep-but-valid graphs. Convert the DFS to an explicit stack-based iterative traversal so fsck is bounded only by memory.
title: fsck cycle detection uses recursive DFS
